current.summary: Designed hooks empirical testing framework: community checklist (modeled on agentskillimplementation.com/checks/), benchmark test scripts, and syllago dogfood plan. Researched agent pricing — 8-10 agents testable for free.

activeWork.description: Create 3 artifacts: hooks behavior checklist (repo + community site proposal), benchmark test script design, and syllago dogfood plan for deploying test hooks across agents
activeWork.progress: Research phase COMPLETE — all hooks behavior data validated. Pricing research COMPLETE — know which agents are free/paid. Dachary's skill checks format analyzed. Three artifacts scoped but not yet drafted.

recentDecisions: Checklist format follows Dachary's agentskillimplementation.com/checks/ pattern: ID + category + 'what it checks' + 'why it matters'
Three artifacts: (1) checklist doc + community site proposal, (2) benchmark test script design with multi-persona review, (3) syllago dogfood plan
Test scripts should be canonical hooks that syllago installs on each agent — dogfooding the converter
8-10 agents can be tested for free: Gemini CLI, Cursor, Windsurf, Kiro, Cline, OpenCode, gptme, Pi, possibly Amazon Q and Augment
Claude Code and GitHub Copilot products require payment ($10-20/mo)
Roo Code has no hook system yet — skip
nextSteps: Draft the hooks behavior checklist (~30 checks across 6 categories: event binding, blocking, capabilities, runtime, security, edge cases)
Design benchmark test hook scripts — one canonical hook per check that produces machine-readable pass/fail output
Design syllago dogfood plan — how syllago converts and installs the benchmark hooks on each target agent
Run all 3 artifacts through multi-persona panel review (Spec Author, Hook Author, Agent Developer, syllago User, QA Engineer)
Commit hooks behavior research (15 files from this session) + the 3 new artifacts

context.notes: This is a follow-on from the hooks-behavior-research handoff which completed all 6 phases of the research plan. The research produced 2,388 lines of validated findings across 15 files covering 8 spec agents + 6 non-spec agents. The empirical testing framework will use those findings as the expected behavior baseline — the checklist checks are derived directly from the research. The syllago dogfood angle is particularly valuable: writing canonical test hooks in syllago format and using syllago's converter to deploy them to each agent validates both the hooks spec AND the converter implementation simultaneously. Key reference: Dachary's skill checks at agentskillimplementation.com/checks/ uses a diagnostic checklist format with check IDs, categories, and 'why it matters' explanations — we're adapting this for hooks.
